name: French Onion Soup
tagline: Caramelized onions, beef broth, gruyère-crowned crouton
highlight: Forty minutes of patient stirring = mahogany onions

hero.alt: French onion soup with bubbling cheese-topped crouton
hero.caption: french onion — caramel and gruyère

healthFacts: Naturally nut-free; built on onions, broth, bread, and cheese.
Onions release quercetin during long, slow cooking — anti-inflammatory.
Gruyère is naturally low in lactose due to long aging.

steps.title: Slow caramelize
steps.detail: Melt butter and oil in a heavy pot. Add onions and a pinch of salt. Cook over medium-low 40 minutes, stirring every few minutes, until deep mahogany.
steps.tip: Brown bits sticking? Splash water and scrape — that's flavor.

steps.title: Deglaze with wine
steps.detail: Pour in wine, scrape the fond, and reduce until nearly dry, ~3 minutes.
steps.tip: Use a dry white you'd drink — sweet wine will throw off the balance.

steps.title: Simmer with broth
steps.detail: Add stock, bay leaf, thyme. Bring to a simmer and cook gently 30 minutes. Salt to taste.
steps.tip: Use the best stock you can — it's the bulk of the flavor.

steps.title: Crouton and broil
steps.detail: Ladle into oven-safe bowls. Top each with toast and a heap of gruyère. Broil 2–3 minutes until cheese is bubbly and browned.
steps.tip: Set bowls on a sheet pan — overflowing cheese is real.
